summary: Created MPI-162 — shrink LTX text-encoder: A/B GGUF Gemma Q4_K_M(7.3GB)+Q5_K_M(8.44GB) + abliterated LoRA vs the 14.5GB fp8 heretic. The real LTX speed lever (slowness is RAM/load-bound, Gemma=half the footprint). UNLOCK: official workflow uses STOCK gemma + abliterated LoRA (not baked heretic) so any GGUF gemma works; and ships fp4 itself (contradicts our fp4-hurts note). DualCLIPLoader(GGUF) inherits the ltxv type from the stock node -> ltxv very likely supported, mixed gguf+bf16 likely works. bartowski URLs in card. Builder-Pod A/B: quality (faces/prompt/uncensor) + speed/RAM incl low-RAM Pod.

Refined (user): abliterated LoRA is UNTESTABLE for us — prior tests (gore/NSFW) found no pin-downable difference; only firm finding was specialized-Gemma >= LoRA. So the LoRA is a SHIP-IT HEDGE, NOT a test gate. Keep it in (official-workflow wiring, ~0.5GB, uncensor insurance moving off heretic Gemma), do NOT block on isolating its effect. The real decider = stock-GGUF-Gemma + LoRA matches fp8-heretic quality while ~7GB lighter + faster.

Picked up MPI-162 — scope narrowed by user to LOCAL Gemma-quant test (Pod later). LoRA already tested (heretic abliterated, 628MB on disk). Task now: download bartowski Q4_K_M(7.3GB)+Q5_K_M(8.44GB) GGUF to G:/CubricModels/text_encoders/ so user can wire them in the editable template DualCLIPLoaderGGUF dropdown and eyeball quality vs the 14.5GB fp8 heretic baseline. Desk-verified: clip_gguf folder = text_encoders (ComfyUI-GGUF nodes.py:33), DualCLIPLoaderGGUF class present locally, abliterated-heretic LoRA source = official Comfy-Org/ltx-2 repo.

SUPERSEDED / parked in doing (user, 2026-06-30). The original premise (engine-split GGUF-Gemma + abliterated-LoRA hedge, fp8-Pod vs bf16-local) is OBSOLETE under a bigger restructure now planned: (1) a new Detailer LoRA added to the workflow for quality; (2) baked LoRA-MERGE into the model weights (same pattern as other shipped models) instead of runtime loaders; (3) low/balanced/high VRAM TIERS — workflows split by tier, so local and Pod no longer need DIFFERENT weights (the bf16-local/GGUF-Pod asymmetry that drove this card dissolves). User will author/restructure new workflows. CONCLUDED FINDING THAT SURVIVES: stock-Gemma Q4_K_M GGUF (bartowski) = ZERO visible quality diff vs the 14.5GB fp8 heretic (user A/B, local, this session) AND loads/gens fine via DualCLIPLoaderGGUF with mixed gguf-clip1 + bf16-projection-clip2, type=ltxv — so a ~7GB-lighter Gemma is PROVEN viable; carry this into the tier restructure. NO CODE SHIPPED: dependencies.js / models.js / workflow JSONs were NOT edited (only inspected). R2 ORPHANS PENDING GC (recorded in docs/builder/04-add-models.md GC ledger, cubric-models bucket): Q4 GGUF 7.3GB sha fc57f67e + heretic-abliterated LoRA 599MB sha caa438a3 — staged before the cancel, unreferenced, delete after the restructure settles encoder/LoRA delivery. Both verified live on models.cubric.studio. Card stays in doing as a knowledge anchor for the restructure.

Won't-do. Gemma clip DECIDED = fp4_mixed shared (all engines+tiers). No bf16/GGUF for the text encoder. Card premise (GGUF-shrink Gemma) dead; per-tier Gemma re-decide cancelled. R2 orphans now dead-safe to GC (Q4 GGUF 7.3GB fc57f67e + heretic LoRA 599MB caa438a3), user go needed.
Archived completed card during board cleanup.
